refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall minimize energy consumption per ton moved to meet or exceed the customer-defined energy efficiency target.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the high-level FR into a measurable and actionable sub-FR focused on minimizing energy per ton moved aligns with the acceptance criteria and enables targeted design and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure total energy used and tons moved over the evaluation period and verify energy_per_ton ≤ E_target using telemetry data.
sub_frs: Minimize energy consumption during vehicle operation
Optimize task scheduling to reduce energy usage
Monitor and report energy efficiency KPI

candidate_dps: Real-time energy monitoring and reporting system
selected_dp.selected_dp: Combination of energy-efficient vehicle powertrain design, adaptive task scheduling algorithm, and real-time energy monitoring and reporting system

potential_sources: Energy monitoring depends on vehicle telemetry
Task scheduling interacts with vehicle operation
coupling_check.notes: DPs are logically coupled but can be modularized with clear interfaces; coupling is manageable.

assumptions_risks: Assumes accurate and timely telemetry data is available
Risk of suboptimal scheduling if task data is delayed
Energy efficiency improvements depend on vehicle hardware capabilities
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider refining task scheduling DP further if integration complexity increases.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Implements energy-efficient powertrain design and provides telemetry data

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle sends energy usage and operational data to other modules

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives task schedules from Teleoperation module

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment of powertrain DP; low diffusion risk
Holds task scheduling DP; moderate diffusion due to interaction with Vehicle
Minimal relevance to energy efficiency DP
High-level integration but poor containment of detailed DPs

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Vehicle module best encapsulates energy efficiency related DPs with minimal diffusion and clear telemetry interface.
containment_risks: Potential overlap of task scheduling logic between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules
Telemetry data latency affecting real-time monitoring accuracy
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Clarify task scheduling ownership between Vehicle and Teleoperation to reduce diffusion.

fr_dp_gate.reason: FR refinement is clear and measurable; selected DPs logically satisfy sub-FRs with manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Task scheduling DP diffusion between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Vehicle module shows strong containment for powertrain DP; task scheduling requires coordination.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification via telemetry and tasking interfaces is feasible with existing known interfaces.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of powertrain DP to Vehicle; task scheduling allocation needs refinement.

recommended_revisions: Refine task scheduling DP and IM boundary to reduce diffusion risk
Establish clear interface protocols for telemetry and tasking data exchange

decision.justification: Overall decomposition is sound but task scheduling DP and its IM assignment require clearer boundaries to reduce diffusion and improve ownership clarity.
next_level_sub_frs: Minimize energy consumption during vehicle operation
Optimize task scheduling to reduce energy usage
Monitor and report energy efficiency KPI

revise.why: To clarify task scheduling logic and ownership, reduce diffusion, and improve modularity.

assumptions: Telemetry data is accurate and timely
open_risks: Task scheduling diffusion between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules
evidence_needed: Telemetry data latency and accuracy metrics
Task scheduling interface definitions and ownership agreements
